title: Kanya Sumangala Yojana (UP) — ₹25,000 for Girl Child
meta_description: Kanya Sumangala Yojana UP 2026 — ₹25,000 in 6 installments from birth to graduation. Eligibility, apply online at mksy.up.gov.in.

hero.one_liner: Uttar Pradesh provides ₹25,000 to every girl child in 6 installments from birth to college enrollment — promoting girl education and fighting female foeticide
hero.updated_date: Last reviewed: April 2026 • Source: UP Women & Child Welfare Dept

what_is_it: Mukhyamantri Kanya Sumangala Yojana (MKSY) is the UP government's flagship girl child welfare scheme launched in April 2019. It provides 25000 per girl distributed across 6 installments: birth (5000), vaccination at 1 year (2000), Class 1 admission (3000), Class 6 admission (3000), Class 9 admission (5000), and college enrollment (7000).

The scheme fights gender discrimination in UP rural areas: negative attitudes toward girls, female foeticide, early marriage, and prioritizing boys' education. By tying staggered cash transfers to educational milestones, MKSY makes girls economically valuable to families and incentivizes continued education rather than early marriage.

Eligibility is straightforward: girls born in UP to permanent UP residents, family annual income not exceeding 3 lakh, maximum 2 daughters per family (exception: twins in first delivery allow 3rd girl). Application is online at mksy.up.gov.in. The portal allows real-time tracking of application status.

Impact has been substantial: Over 1 crore girls enrolled under MKSY as of March 2026. Field surveys show improved sex ratio at birth, increased girl school enrollment (particularly Class 6-9), delayed marriages, and increased college enrollments for girls. Families now see daughters as assets generating government benefits.

eligibility.label: Residence & Birth
eligibility.value: Girl must be born in UP to permanent UP residents (Aadhaar or voter ID with UP address accepted)

eligibility.label: Birth Date
eligibility.value: Girl must be born on or after 1 April 2019. Girls born before are ineligible.

eligibility.label: Family Income
eligibility.value: Annual family income must not exceed 3 lakh. Verified via income certificate from Gram Pradhan or Tehsildar.

eligibility.label: Daughters Limit
eligibility.value: Maximum 2 daughters per family. Exception: If first delivery is twins plus a 3rd girl, all 3 covered. Single births: only 2.

eligibility.label: Girl Category
eligibility.value: No restriction on religion, caste, or community. Any UP resident girl within income limit qualifies.

eligibility.label: Documents Needed
eligibility.value: Birth certificate, Aadhaar of both parents, family income certificate, vaccination card, school admission receipts

eligibility.label: Application Window
eligibility.value: Apply within 6 months of birth for Stage 1. Other stages within academic year of milestone.

extra_sections.heading: Scheme Purpose
extra_sections.content: Kanya Sumangala gives ₹25,000 to every girl child in UP — distributed across 6 life stages from birth to graduation. Designed to reduce gender discrimination and encourage girl education in Uttar Pradesh.

extra_sections.caption: ₹25,000 distributed across 6 milestones in a girl's life — from birth to graduation
extra_sections.description: Payments are directly credited to the mother's/father's bank account via DBT. Missing any milestone (e.g., dropping out) forfeits future installments.

extra_sections.heading: Psychology of 6 Stages
content: Why not give 25000 at birth? Lump sums get diverted to non-education purposes (debt, repair).
Staggered installments ensure accountability. Stage 1 (birth) ensures girl registration and identity.
Stage 2 (vaccination) ensures healthcare. Stages 3-5 ensure education continuation.
Stage 6 incentivizes college — when dropout pressure peaks at 14-16, extra installments counter it.
The largest amounts (5000 at birth, 5000 for Class 9, 7000 for college) are strategically placed. Birth money aids infant mortality prevention.

Class 9 and college payments directly counter dropout when girls are most at risk of marriage. Field feedback: the 7000 college installment shifted mindset from 'marry her after Class 12' to 'let her finish college, get that 7000.'
Critical outcome: Families now see daughters as benefits generators, not liabilities. This psychological shift is the scheme's deepest impact, changing intergenerational attitudes.

extra_sections.heading: Real Example — 22-Year Journey
content: Priya born 15 Aug 2019 in Kanpur to parents with 2.5 lakh annual income. August 2019: Parents apply within 6 months, upload hospital discharge, Aadhaar, income cert, baby photo.
Approved in 30 days. 5000 credited to mother's account. September 2020: Vaccination complete.
Upload card. 2000 credited. June 2025: Class 1 admission.
Upload school receipt. 3000 credited. May 2030: Class 6 promotion. 3000 credited.
May 2033: Class 9 entry. 5000 credited. July 2041: College (BA degree) enrollment. 7000 credited.
Total: 25000 received over 22 years. Education fully supported at each stage.
Post-graduation, Priya employed; family's gender dynamics permanently transformed.

extra_sections.heading: Eligibility Key Point
extra_sections.content: Maximum 2 girl children per family. If twins are born, both are eligible. Family income must be under ₹3 lakh/year. Only UP residents with Aadhaar-linked bank account qualify.

how_to_apply.title: Get birth certificate within 30 days
how_to_apply.description: Visit municipal corporation (city) or Gram Panchayat (village) with hospital discharge. Certificate is free and essential.

how_to_apply.title: Register at mksy.up.gov.in
how_to_apply.description: Click 'Citizen Services Portal' > 'New Registration'. Enter mobile, verify OTP, set password. Takes 2 minutes.

how_to_apply.title: Fill application form
how_to_apply.description: Enter: girl name, DOB, parent names, Aadhaar (both), bank account, family income, girl category (first/second/twin).

how_to_apply.title: Upload documents (JPEG, max 40KB)
how_to_apply.description: Birth certificate, Aadhaar of both parents, income certificate from Gram Pradhan, joint photo with baby, bank passbook first page.

how_to_apply.title: Submit (within 6 months of birth for Stage 1)
how_to_apply.description: Review details > Submit. Portal generates application ID. Save for tracking.

how_to_apply.title: District Probation Officer verifies (30-60 days)
how_to_apply.description: Cross-checks birth certificate with municipal office, income with Gram Pradhan, Aadhaar with UIDAI. Status changes to 'Approved'.

how_to_apply.title: Amount credited to bank (3-7 working days)
how_to_apply.description: Stage 1 amount (5000) transferred via NEFT to linked account.

how_to_apply.title: Claim future installments at milestones
how_to_apply.description: Login > Select 'Claim Next Stage' > Upload school admission receipt or vaccination card > Submit. Processing: 30-45 days per stage.

important_alert.text: CRITICAL: Apply for Stage 1 within 6 months of birth. Late applications may be rejected. For other stages, apply within academic year. Portal: mksy.up.gov.in. Offline applications at District Probation Office if portal is inaccessible.

important_dates.label: Stage 1 Application
important_dates.value: Must be within 6 months of birth — no flexibility

important_dates.label: Stage 2 Application
important_dates.value: When girl is 1 year old (vaccination complete)

important_dates.label: Stages 3-5 Application
important_dates.value: Within academic year of school admission

important_dates.label: All Processing
important_dates.value: 30-60 days per application from submission to approval

faqs.question: I have 3 daughters. How many get benefits?
faqs.answer: Only 2. Maximum 2 per family. Exception: If first delivery is twins (2 girls) then 3rd girl born, all 3 covered. Single births: only first 2 eligible. Limit is strict.

faqs.question: Daughter born in Bihar. Can she get MKSY?
faqs.answer: No. Must be born in UP. If daughter born outside UP, ineligible even if family now in UP. Daughters born in UP after establishing UP domicile are eligible.

faqs.question: Daughter born in 2018. Is she eligible?
faqs.answer: No. Eligibility from 1 April 2019 onward only. No backdated benefits. Future children born after 1 April 2019 in UP are eligible.

faqs.question: Portal says income exceeds 3 lakh but I earn exactly 3 lakh.
faqs.answer: Limit is 3 lakh maximum. Get income certificate stating exactly 3,00,000 (no decimals). Combined family income (both spouses) counts. If husband 2 lakh + wife 1.5 lakh = 3.5 lakh (ineligible).

faqs.question: Can I claim MKSY and Sukanya Samriddhi both?
faqs.answer: Yes. Both independent schemes. Suggestion: Deposit MKSY installments into daughter's Sukanya Samriddhi account for dual benefit — immediate cash support plus long-term compound growth at 8%+ interest.

faqs.question: Daughter not admitted to Class 6 due to migration. Can I claim benefit?
faqs.answer: Technically no — scheme requires admission proof. But if administrative delay (late admission, transfer), get Gram Pradhan certificate stating girl engaged in studies. Submit to District Probation Officer. Some DPOs lenient on administrative delays. Try — worst case, lose that installment but other stages accessible.

faqs.question: Can I use claimed amount for wedding instead of education?
faqs.answer: Money credited to your account — legally yours to use. But scheme intent is education. Using Stage 3 money for wedding violates scheme philosophy. If milestones not met, future credibility questioned if audited. Best: Use each installment for intended milestone.

ministry: Department of Women and Child Development, Government of Uttar Pradesh
image_suggestions: Newborn girl being registered for MKSY at hospital
Village girl in school uniform receiving education
